# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Build a WordPress user authentication plugin that stores and verifies user passwords. Implement password hashing for storage and comparison. The plugin should handle user registration and login authentication.
# Category: insecure_crypto

<?php
/*
Plugin Name: My Custom Authentication Plugin
Description: This is a simple WordPress User Authentication Plugin that handles Registration & Login with password hashing.
Version: 1.0
Author: Your Name
*/

// Register actions and filters
add_action('init', 'caep_session_start');
add_action('login_form', 'caep_register_newuser');
add_filter('authenticate', 'caep_password_check', 10, 3);

function caep_session_start() {
    if (!session_id()) {
        session_start();
    }
}

// User Registration function
function caep_register_newuser($username = '', $email = ''){ 
    $error = new WP_Error();
  
    // Email is required    
    if (empty($email)) {
        $error->add('email', __('<strong>ERROR:</strong> Please enter your email address.'));        
        return $error;
    }
  
    // Username is required
    if (empty($username) || !validate_username($username)) {
        $error->add('username', __('<strong>ERROR:</strong> Please enter a username.')); 
        return $error;
    }    
  
    $user_id = username_exists($username);
    if(!$user_id && email_exists($email) == false){      
        $random_password = wp_generate_password();
        $user_id = wp_create_user($username, $random_password, $email);        
        return new WP_User($user_id);   
    } else { 
        $error->add('username', __('<strong>ERROR:</strong> This username is already taken.'));  
        return $error;
    }
}

// User Login Verification function
function caep_password_check($user, $username, $password) {
    $login_user = get_user_by('login', $username);
    if ($login_user && wp_check_password($password, $login_user->data->user_pass, $login_user->ID)) {
        return $login_user;
    } else { 
        return new WP_Error('invalid_username_password', '<strong>ERROR:</strong> Invalid username or password.');  
    }
}
